title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] swagger: remove name wildcards

comments.body:
Thanks @tmds 
I believe the wild cards were added for a reason, but I will let @jwhonce or @baude comment on that.

Please sign your commits.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force

> I believe the wild cards were added for a reason, but I will let @jwhonce or @baude comment on that.

@jwhonce @baude do the wild cards serve some purpose?

> @jwhonce @baude do the wild cards serve some purpose?

@jwhonce @baude ping.

I've added the 'NO TESTS' and rebased.

Can this be merged, unless someone remembers why the wildcards are there?
Seems like all of the tests do not like this change?
The swagger errors look legitimate. I'm not sure about the rest.

I think I'm to eagerly removing these wild cards.

I care about these:
```
// swagger:operation GET /libpod/generate/{name:.*}/systemd libpod libpodGenerateSystemd
```
And probably the test failures are due to changing these:
```
r.HandleFunc(VersionedPath("/libpod/generate/{name:.*}/systemd"), s.APIHandler(libpod.GenerateSystemd)).Methods(http.MethodGet)
```
@mheon I've limited my change to comments. Can you take a look at the CI tests? I don't understand what they're trying to tell me.

Ah - you're changing `vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/types/types.go`. That's vendored library code from `docker/docker` (we grabbed their API structs to ensure exact compatibility). We can't change that directly ourselves, as it's source from the `docker/docker` (well, `moby/moby` now) repository. I recommend backing out changes to that file; if you'd still like to go ahead you can submit them to `moby/moby` and we can include the fixed version once they are merged there.
@mheon I've removed the changes to `vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/types/types.go`.
Ack. We should be good to merge, then.
@jwhonce PTAL
@jwhonce @mheon can you help me understand what CI is unhappy about?

@tmds your change is incomplete. The CI failure is my last-minute validation script, it is complaining that the parameters are inconsistent between comments and code. For instance:
```
- 	// swagger:operation GET /images/{name}/get compat exportImage
+ 	// ................. ... /images/{name:.*}/get ......
  	r.Handle(VersionedPath("/images/{name:.*}/get"), s.APIHandler(compat.ExportImage)).Methods(http.MethodGet)
```
That is saying that the swagger comment claims `{name}`, but just a few lines below the actual code is registering `{name:.*}`. My code is trying to enforce that the swagger comment be identical to the `r.Handle()` code.

> My code is trying to enforce that the swagger comment be identical to the r.Handle() code.

@edsantiago I'm trying to get rid of the `.*` that end up in the swagger. I think I need to remove them from the comments, but keep them in the `r.Handle` calls. I think we need to update the expectation of the test. Does that make sense?

I don't know anything at all about swagger; I just know that for a long time we had inconsistencies between the code and the documentation, and my script fixed those. I will do whatever @jwhonce and @baude recommend.
@jwhonce @baude I removed the `.*` from the comments, but kept them in the r.Handle calls. I think we need to update the expectation of the test. Does that make sense?
ping @jwhonce @baude

> Is this a stylistic change you want or is this blocking some tool?

It breaks code generation using https://github.com/RicoSuter/NSwag.
Can we remove them from the comments, so they don't end up in the swagger?
@tmds I'll take a look at our validator to see how we can extend it to cover this use case.
@edsantiago I've applied the changes. Let's see what CI makes of it.

It's going to pass. BTW you can run the test manually in your own checked-out tree:
```console
$ ./hack/swagger-check
```
It runs in less than a second, and requires only `perl` and `File::Find`, which I believe are standard in all distros I know.
LGTM but I'd like @jwhonce to have the final merge say-so. @tmds thank you for your patience!
/lgtm

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** Approval requirements bypassed by manually added approval. This pull-request has been approved by: *tmds* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago thanks for your assistance!
